name: Norrbotten County (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the blue triangles drawn from the historic arms of Lapland
the gold triangles drawn from the historic Bothnian arms

description: A square flag divided per saltire blue and gold, charged centrally with the composite arms of Norrbotten County.
history: Norrbotten County's arms were granted in 1995, combining elements from the constituent historic provinces of Lapland and Vasterbotten; the flag is the heraldic banner of those arms.

features.symbolism: the field is divided per saltire, alternating the blue and gold of the constituent provincial arms (Lapland and the Bothnian region) brought together in the county arms.
a composite shield combining symbols of the constituent historic provinces, including a wild man (representing Lapland) and an arm holding an arrow (representing the Bothnian frontier).
